rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports, and in implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for structural contracts, implementations for binding contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure that all AGREE expressions reference only visible features and data types from the AADL model.
Avoid generating assumptions or guarantees for data that is not explicitly defined or visible in the AADL architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should use visible identifiers and valid AADL data types.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not assume or guarantee values for ports or properties that are not explicitly defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
AGREE-specific property and data type handling
